title: Ensure That the Log Metric Filter and Alerts Exist for Cloud  Storage IAM Permission Changes

input_row.title: Ensure That the Log Metric Filter and Alerts Exist for Cloud  Storage IAM Permission Changes
input_row.description: It is recommended that a metric filter and alarm be established for Cloud Storage Bucket IAM changes.
input_row.rationale: Monitoring changes to cloud storage bucket permissions may reduce the time needed to detect and correct permissions on sensitive cloud storage buckets and objects inside the bucket.
input_row.audit: From Google Cloud Console Ensure the prescribed log metric is present: 1. For each project that contains cloud storage buckets, go to Logging/Logs- based Metrics by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/logs/metrics. 2. In the User-defined Metrics section, ensure at least one metric is present with the filter text: resource.type="gcs_bucket" AND protoPayload.methodName="storage.setIamPermissions" Ensure that the prescribed alerting policy is present: 3. Go to Alerting by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/monitoring/alerting. 4. Under the Policies section, ensure that at least one alert policy exists for the log metric above. Clicking on the policy should show that it is configured with a condition. For example, Violates when: Any logging.googleapis.com/user/ stream is above a threshold of 0 for greater than 0 seconds means that the alert will trigger for any new owner change. Verify that the chosen alerting thresholds make sense for the user's organization. 5. Ensure that the appropriate notifications channels have been set up. From Google Cloud CLI Ensure that the prescribed log metric is present: 1. List the log metrics: gcloud logging metrics list --format json 2. Ensure that the output contains at least one metric with the filter set to: resource.type=gcs_bucket AND protoPayload.methodName="storage.setIamPermissions" 3. Note the value of the property metricDescriptor.type for the identified metric, in the format logging.googleapis.com/user/. Ensure the prescribed alerting policy is present: 4. List the alerting policies: gcloud alpha monitoring policies list --format json 5. Ensure that the output contains an least one alert policy where: • conditions.conditionThreshold.filter is set to metric.type=\"logging.googleapis.com/user/\" • AND enabled is set to true
input_row.remediation: From Google Cloud Console Create the prescribed log metric: 1. Go to Logging/Logs-based Metrics by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/logs/metrics and click "CREATE METRIC". 2. Click the down arrow symbol on the Filter Bar at the rightmost corner and select Convert to Advanced Filter. 3. Clear any text and add: resource.type="gcs_bucket" AND protoPayload.methodName="storage.setIamPermissions" 4. Click Submit Filter. Display logs appear based on the filter text entered by the user. 5. In the Metric Editor menu on right, fill out the name field. Set Units to 1 (default) and Type to Counter. This ensures that the log metric counts the number of log entries matching the user's advanced logs query. 6. Click Create Metric. Create the prescribed Alert Policy: 1. Identify the newly created metric under the section User-defined Metrics at https://console.cloud.google.com/logs/metrics. 2. Click the 3-dot icon in the rightmost column for the new metric and select Create alert from Metric. A new page appears. 3. Fill out the alert policy configuration and click Save. Choose the alerting threshold and configuration that makes sense for the user's organization. For example, a threshold of zero(0) for the most recent value will ensure that a notification is triggered for every owner change in the project: Set `Aggregator` to `Count` Set `Configuration`: - Condition: above - Threshold: 0 - For: most recent value 4. Configure the desired notifications channels in the section Notifications. 5. Name the policy and click Save. From Google Cloud CLI Create the prescribed Log Metric: • Use the command: gcloud beta logging metrics create Create the prescribed alert policy: • Use the command: gcloud alpha monitoring policies create
input_row.impact: Enabling of logging may result in your project being charged for the additional logs usage. These charges could be significant depending on the size of the organization.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The audit section of the control includes detailed steps using the Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) and GCP CLI commands, confirming that verification can be conducted programmatically.
- The control involves checking standard GCP service properties like logs-based metrics and alerting policies, which are accessible via the GCP APIs.
- The requirements focus solely on metrics and alerts related to IAM permissions for Cloud Storage, which are standard-related checks that can be automated.

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_logging_metric_alert_policy_storage_bucket_iam_changes

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
1. **List log metrics**: Use the command `gcloud logging metrics list --format json` to verify if any log metric exists with the filter `resource.type=gcs_bucket AND protoPayload.methodName="storage.setIamPermissions"`.
   - **Expected output**: The output must contain at least one metric where the `filter` matches the specified conditions.
  
2. **Check log metric type**: From the identified metric, validate that `metricDescriptor.type` is of the format `logging.googleapis.com/user/`.
   - **Expected output**: Ensure that the property `metricDescriptor.type` contains the correct value format herein specified.

3. **List alerting policies**: Use the command `gcloud alpha monitoring policies list --format json` to fetch alerting policies and check for at least one policy that meets the conditions specified.
   - **Conditions to check**:
     - Check if any alert policy has `conditions.conditionThreshold.filter` set to `metric.type="logging.googleapis.com/user/"`.
     - Ensure that the alert policy's `enabled` property is set to `true`.

4. **Pass/Fail Criteria**:
   - A "Pass" indicates that there is at least one log metric configured correctly and at least one corresponding alerting policy that is enabled.
   - A "Fail" indicates that either the log metric is not present or the alerting policy does not meet the specified criteria. 

By implementing these automated checks, the compliance control regarding Cloud Storage IAM permission changes can be effectively monitored, ensuring adherence to the best practices outlined in the CIS GCP Foundation Benchmark.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE
- The classification as "Automated" is correct. The audit can be conducted using GCP APIs and the Google Cloud SDK (gcloud), which supports programmatic verification of log metrics and alerting policies.
- The use of GCP SDK capabilities and CLI commands is appropriate for this control, as it involves checking configurations that are accessible through these tools.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 8
- The automation details are mostly technically correct. The use of `gcloud logging metrics list` and `gcloud alpha monitoring policies list` commands is valid for retrieving log metrics and alerting policies.
- The command to list alerting policies should be updated to use `gcloud monitoring policies list` instead of `gcloud alpha monitoring policies list`, as the alpha command may not be necessary or available in all environments.
- The program name `gcp_logging_metric_alert_policy_storage_bucket_iam_changes` is descriptive but could be more concise.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**
- Ensure the use of the most stable and widely available gcloud commands. The alpha command for listing monitoring policies should be replaced with the standard command if available.
- Consider including additional checks for the completeness of the alerting policy, such as verifying the notification channels configured for alerts.
- The program name could be improved for clarity and brevity. Suggested name: `gcp_logging_alert_iam_permission_changes_storage`.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH
- The assessment is well-structured and uses appropriate GCP tools to automate the compliance check. The minor issues identified do not significantly impact the overall validity of the approach.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:**
- Modify: Update the command for listing alerting policies to use `gcloud monitoring policies list` instead of the alpha version. Consider refining the program name for clarity and brevity.

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:** Both AI opinions agree that the audit can be automated using GCP APIs and the Google Cloud SDK (gcloud). The control involves checking configurations for log metrics and alerting policies, which are accessible through these tools. The necessary commands and API calls are available to verify the presence of the required log metric and alerting policy, making automation feasible.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:** 
gcp_logging_alert_iam_permission_changes_storage

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- GCP Resource/Service: Cloud Storage Buckets
- API/CLI Method: Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) commands
- Properties to Check: 
  - Log Metrics: Use `gcloud logging metrics list --format json` to check for metrics with the filter `resource.type="gcs_bucket" AND protoPayload.methodName="storage.setIamPermissions"`.
  - Alerting Policies: Use `gcloud monitoring policies list --format json` to verify the presence of alerting policies related to IAM permission changes.
- Pass Criteria: At least one log metric and one alerting policy exist with the specified conditions.
- Fail Criteria: Absence of the required log metric or alerting policy.

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:** Both AI opinions were in agreement about the automated approach, with minor suggestions for improvement in command usage and program naming. I have adopted the more concise program name suggested by the second AI opinion.

**SOURCE:** CIS_Google_Cloud_Platform_Foundation_Benchmark_v4_0_0
